### Session a3104c40-38d7-478c-9cf1-53aa8fd8793d - 2026-07-13T14:24:31.657Z

- User asked for status update on Jobber Optimo route automation (as of 2026-07-13)
- Claude Code reported 07-06 board-scramble fully recovered with root cause identified: OptimoRoute prioritizes C-level orders earlier in day, causing ~20% worse routes (~20% performance delta); all four write paths (`push-week.mjs`, `apply-grid.mjs`, `mirror-history.mjs`, `mirror-lastweek.mjs`) now use uniform priority M, and 70 stray C/H orders on live board were normalized
- Week of 07-14 to 07-17 re-planned and written to Jobber with projected gains of −385 km and −6.1 hours; all 417 time write-backs verified; Cory's Monday separately hand-anchored with real OSRM drive times
- New operational policy locked: all orders priority M always; real customer promises only via `timeWindows`; visit times/windows never carry week-to-week
- n8n workflows remain off (v1 deactivated); all current operations are Claude-driven scripts, not unattended automation
- Outstanding work: WF-1/WF-2 rebuild per v2 architecture including daily drift check (Saturday found 3 new Jobber bookings absent in OptimoRoute); pushing week of 07-20 to OptimoRoute by Friday; arrival-window sweep across ~5k jobs
- Documentation gap flagged: existing brief states priority C policy, contradicts new all-M policy; should be corrected before rebuild to prevent reintroducing bug

### Session a3104c40-38d7-478c-9cf1-53aa8fd8793d - 2026-07-13T14:28:08.666Z

- User asked how new jobs entered for Tuesday–Friday are being handled, noting they're manually entering jobs today and Monday but want to understand the process for the rest of the week.
- Claude Code explained the current drift gap: new jobs land in Jobber but don't reach OptimoRoute because the OR plan (07-14..17) was locked Saturday; cited example job #8158 (booked in Jobber, missing from OR, only caught via manual diff).
- Provided a proven interim workflow: daily diff of Jobber vs OR → create missing orders in OR with locks → re-plan affected days only → write times back to Jobber.
- Noted timing constraint: Jobber emails arrival windows at ~14:00 PT on D-1, so each day freezes by 2 PM the prior day; daily sweep must run midday to catch next day's additions before email freeze.
- Offered two options: (1) manual drift-check runs on request (~12:30 PT each day), or (2) local cron daemon (noted as stopgap, requires manual restart after reboots).
- Recommended durable fix deferred to WF-1/WF-2 rebuild where daily drift-check becomes automatic step.
- Asked whether to run the first sweep now to catch bookings since Saturday.

### Session 06959296-f7bb-4e89-a766-1e7d956a124d - 2026-07-13T14:31:17.871Z

- User requested Gmail triage of Roy's mailbox (roy@atpbos.com): fetch new mail, classify per rules, archive junk/FYI, label needs-action items, and draft replies in Roy's voice — following hard rules (never send, never delete, archive only).

- Claude Code ran step 1 (`node scripts/gmail/gmail-fetch.cjs --full`) to fetch new inbox messages; command failed immediately with missing credential error: `GMAIL_CLIENT_ID` not in .env.

- Stopped execution at step 1 per hard rule ("if a step errors, stop and report") — no mail fetched, no labels applied, no archives, no drafts created; steps 2–5 never ran.

- Diagnosed root cause: the cron is trying to access Roy's mailbox from Spencer's Got Moles install (separate install, separate .env) with no Google Workspace OAuth credentials set up — missing GMAIL_CLIENT_ID, GMAIL_CLIENT_SECRET, GMAIL_REFRESH_TOKEN.

- Identified pattern: this mirrors two existing gaps already logged in memory — meeting-clips cron (Notion token in Roy/Elevate 360's workspace) and Gmail triage (no GMAIL_* OAuth keys noted as missing on this install).

- Offered two decision paths: either add Roy's Gmail OAuth credentials to this install's .env and run `gmail-auth.cjs` to mint refresh token, or disable the cron here since it belongs on Roy's install by ownership.

- Noted the issue in memory so future runs don't re-diagnose the same credentials error from scratch.

### Session a3104c40-38d7-478c-9cf1-53aa8fd8793d - 2026-07-13T14:47:32.880Z

- User requested scheduling a drift-check job for Jobber⇄OptimoRoute route automation to run every 2 hours between 9am–5pm PT daily.
- Claude Code created `cron/jobs/route-drift-check.md` using the ops-cron skill; the job fires at 9, 11, 1, 3, and 5 PT every day, executing `drift-check.mjs fix` deterministically.
- The cron daemon is running and established as leader; first unattended tick scheduled for 9:00 AM the same day (2026-07-13).
- Auto-fix scope confirmed: new Jobber bookings missing from OptimoRoute are geocoded and created, that day is re-planned with everyone locked to day+tech, and times written back to Jobber; existing orders showing day/tech/time drift are report-only.
- First supervised run already executed and caught two real missing bookings: #6116 (Wed, Spencer, slotted 10:45) and #4979 (Thu, Luke, slotted 11:05); 44 Jobber time adjustments written with zero failures.
- Hard guards are baked into the script: 14:00 PT day-before email freeze, only OR-planned days checked, only own orders touched, max 15 creates per run, abort if re-plan moves any stop to different day/tech.
- Full JSON execution logs saved to `projects/briefs/technician-route-automation/drift-runs/` for detailed review.
- Two operational caveats noted: daemon runtime requires manual `start-crons` restart after reboot (stopgap behavior); new bookings entered after 2 PM tomorrow will land in OR unrouted and flagged for manual handling.

### Session a3104c40-38d7-478c-9cf1-53aa8fd8793d - 2026-07-13T14:49:29.584Z

- Spencer asked whether the cron daemon stopgap is a hard dependency—whether automation only runs if the daemon/PC is actively running.
- Claude Code mapped the full dependency chain: the daemon (pid 28820) executes jobs locally on the PC; if the PC is off/asleep/rebooted, scheduled drift checks stop firing until `start-crons` is run manually.
- Clarified that catch-up logic reschedules missed jobs once the daemon restarts, so a daemon recovery at 2 PM catches the 9/11/1 AM misses—but only after manual restart.
- Contextualized the "stopgap" label: it's a 07-10 hotfix that works (Jobber notes job: 4-for-4), but it's desktop-bound, not server-backed infrastructure.
- Explained the durable solution: WF-1/WF-2 rebuild on n8n Cloud will move drift logic into a server-side workflow independent of PC state, retiring the local cron.
- Offered practical interim guidance: leave PC on during business hours (9–5 PT is when checks matter); restart daemon manually after reboots; optionally flag dead daemons at first session of each day.
- Offered conditional escalation: if Spencer plans extended downtime, prioritize n8n deployment sooner rather than waiting for full WF rebuild.

### Session a3104c40-38d7-478c-9cf1-53aa8fd8793d - 2026-07-13T14:51:37.362Z

- Spencer asked for clarification on what a "daemon" means and whether Visual Studio needs to be kept open for the background drift-check job to run.
- Claude Code explained that a daemon is a background program running invisibly without user interaction — it doesn't require VS, Claude chat, or any visible window to be open.
- Core dependencies clarified: the computer must stay on and awake during work hours; after reboots, the cron job must be restarted manually with "start crons" command (takes seconds).
- Current state confirmed: the daemon is already running (same one that powers the nightly 6:15 PM Jobber notes job), so today's scheduled drift-check ticks (9:00, 11:00, 13:00, 15:00, 17:00 PT) will fire automatically as long as the machine remains powered.
- Future upgrade path noted: eventual migration to n8n (cloud service) would remove even the machine-on/restart dependencies — the job would run unattended daily regardless of local machine state.
